visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Short stay visas (Schengen)
The short-stay visa application must be submitted on a completed application form (original and copy), which can be downloaded free of charge from this webpage or obtained free of charge from Austrian diplomatic missions or consular offices abroad.
When submitting the visa application, the established fee must be paid (generally). This fee is non-refundable and therefore will not be refunded if the visa application is unsuccessful. Under certain circumstances applicants may be entitled to a reduction or may be exempt from paying the fee. Please consult the Diplomatic Missions or Austria Consular Offices. For all other necessary requirements, please consult the Diplomatic Mission or Consular Office where the visa application is being made as these may vary according to the reason for the visit and the applicant's country of origin.
The processing time for short-stay (Schengen) visa applications is a minimum of 15 calendar days from the date of submission. In specific cases, the processing period may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days, especially if a more detailed examination of the application or additional documentation is required.
If the visa is issued, it must be collected in person or collected by a duly authorized representative, within a period of one month from the date that notification is given of the award.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Please Note: In addition to the visa fees payable by all applicants, there is a service charge of €27.92 (equivalent in JOD) per application. 

[Table:  Tourist VIsa]

The Visa fees and service charge can be paid by cash or with credit card. It is subject to change without notice. All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 to 45 calendar days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. Applicants may submit their visa applications up to six months before the planned date of travel, but no later than 15 calendar days prior to the intended travel date.
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Jordan nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Jordan can apply for a visa at the Austria Visa Application Centre in Amman.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Jordan nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Jordan can apply for a visa at the Austria Visa Application Centre in Amman.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees
Please Note: In addition to the visa fees payable by all applicants, there is a service charge of €27.92 (equivalent in JOD) per application. 

[Table:  Tourist VIsa]

The Visa fees and service charge can be paid by cash or with credit card. It is subject to change without notice. All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Short stay visas (Schengen)
The short-stay visa application must be submitted on a completed application form (original and copy), which can be downloaded free of charge from this webpage or obtained free of charge from Austrian diplomatic missions or consular offices abroad.
When submitting the visa application, the established fee must be paid (generally). This fee is non-refundable and therefore will not be refunded if the visa application is unsuccessful. Under certain circumstances applicants may be entitled to a reduction or may be exempt from paying the fee. Please consult the Diplomatic Missions or Austria Consular Offices. For all other necessary requirements, please consult the Diplomatic Mission or Consular Office where the visa application is being made as these may vary according to the reason for the visit and the applicant's country of origin.
The processing time for short-stay (Schengen) visa applications is a minimum of 15 calendar days from the date of submission. In specific cases, the processing period may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days, especially if a more detailed examination of the application or additional documentation is required.
If the visa is issued, it must be collected in person or collected by a duly authorized representative, within a period of one month from the date that notification is given of the award.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 to 45 calendar days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. Applicants may submit their visa applications up to six months before the planned date of travel, but no later than 15 calendar days prior to the intended travel date.
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
Short stay visas (Schengen)
